name: LinkedIn: My Jobs
summary: List the logged-in user's own LinkedIn jobs (applied / saved / in_progress / archived) read-only and return up to max_jobs rows.
description: Open the LinkedIn My Jobs page (/my-items/saved-jobs/) in the existing session, switch to the requested cardType tab, scroll, and return a flat array of the user's own job rows. Each item carries job_id, title, company, location, a status line (e.g. "Applied 3d ago"), and an absolute /jobs/view/ URL. Returns an empty array on the "No recent job activity" empty state. Read-only: never applies, saves, archives, or messages.

status.description: Which My Jobs tab to read. Maps to the cardType query param. Defaults to applied.

max_jobs.description: Maximum number of job rows to return. Defaults to 25. Hard cap 50.

steps.name: Open the My Jobs page in the existing session (bootstrap tab before execute_javascript)

inputs.script: const c=(v)=>{const t=String(v==null?'':v).trim();return /^\{[a-zA-Z0-9_]+\}$/.test(t)?'':t;}; const raw=(c(arg0)||'applied').toLowerCase(); const map={applied:'APPLIED',saved:'SAVED',in_progress:'IN_PROGRESS',archived:'ARCHIVED'}; const cardType=map[raw]||'APPLIED'; const status=Object.keys(map).find((k)=>map[k]===cardType)||'applied'; const url='https://www.linkedin.com/my-items/saved-jobs/?cardType='+cardType; setTimeout(()=>location.assign(url),0); return {target_url:url,status:status};

steps.name: Map status to cardType and navigate to the requested My Jobs tab
Allow the My Jobs tab to hydrate
Scroll until roughly max_jobs rows are loaded

inputs.script: const c=(v)=>{const t=String(v==null?'':v).trim();return /^\{[a-zA-Z0-9_]+\}$/.test(t)?'':t;}; let max=parseInt(c(arg0),10); if(!Number.isFinite(max)||max<1)max=25; if(max>50)max=50; const norm=(v)=>(v||'').replace(/\u00a0/g,' ').replace(/\s+/g,' ').trim(); const firstLine=(el)=>norm(((el&&(el.innerText||el.textContent))||'').split('\n')[0]); const stripQ=(href)=>{if(!href)return '';try{const u=new URL(href,location.origin);return u.origin+u.pathname;}catch(e){return href;}}; const bodyText=(document.body&&document.body.innerText)||''; if(/No recent job activity/i.test(bodyText))return []; const anchors=Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('a[href*="/jobs/view/"]')); const out=[]; const seen=new Set(); const statusRe=/(applied|saved|viewed|in progress|archived|application|\bago\b)/i; for(const a of anchors){ const href=a.getAttribute('href')||''; const m=href.match(/\/jobs\/view\/(\d+)/); if(!m)continue; const id=m[1]; if(seen.has(id))continue; seen.add(id); let row=a.closest('li')||a.closest('[data-view-name]'); if(!row){row=a;for(let i=0;i<5;i++){if(row.parentElement)row=row.parentElement;else break;}} const rawLines=((row.innerText||row.textContent||'').split('\n')).map((s)=>norm(s)).filter(Boolean); const uniq=[]; for(const l of rawLines){if(uniq[uniq.length-1]!==l)uniq.push(l);} const title=firstLine(a)||uniq[0]||''; const rest=uniq.filter((l)=>l!==title); const status_text=rest.find((l)=>statusRe.test(l))||''; const company=rest.find((l)=>l!==status_text)||''; const location=rest.find((l)=>l!==status_text&&l!==company)||''; out.push({job_id:id,title:title,company:company,location:location,status_text:status_text,job_url:stripQ(href)}); if(out.length>=max)break; } return out;

steps.name: Extract My Jobs rows (job_id, title, company, location, status_text, job_url)

help.summary: List the logged-in user's own LinkedIn jobs read-only and return up to max_jobs rows.

parameters.status: applied | saved | in_progress | archived. Maps to the cardType tab. Default applied.
parameters.max_jobs: Maximum number of job rows to return. Default 25, max 50.

examples.description: Return up to 25 of your applied jobs.

examples.command: rzn-browser run linkedin my-jobs --param status="saved" --param max_jobs=40
examples.description: Return up to 40 of your saved jobs.

returns: Array of job rows: { job_id, title, company, location, status_text, job_url }. Empty array on the "No recent job activity" empty state.
notes: Read-only. Never applies, saves, archives, or messages.
Requires an existing logged-in LinkedIn session.
status maps to cardType (APPLIED / SAVED / IN_PROGRESS / ARCHIVED) inside the JS step.
Card markup is best-effort; title/company/location/status_text degrade gracefully when a line is missing.
